content: Hybrid Planning and Learning Systems can be restructured around a layered memory architecture that dynamically gates curiosity-driven exploration based on active memory retention, turning sparse unlabeled environmental data into structured long-term planning schemas that improve with the agent’s accumulated experience. This design decouples high-stakes long-term goal planning from intrinsic exploration, letting the agent allocate search effort only to under-learned memory schemas rather than wasting computation on random exploration.
